q: What is the Ethereum network and who created it?
lead: Ethereum is an open-source, programmable blockchain supporting smart contracts, dapps, and DAOs, created by Russian-Canadian programmer Vitalik Buterin.
points: Buterin posted the Ethereum whitepaper in November 2013 at age 19.
Its native currency ether (ETH) buys the 'gas' needed to run dapps.
It is the foundation for decentralized finance (DeFi), a disruptive financial innovation.
The Ethereum Foundation officially launched the blockchain on July 30, 2015.

q: What problems did Vitalik Buterin see with the Bitcoin network for the future of the internet of value (Web3)?
lead: Buterin concluded Bitcoin was inefficient, lacked scalability, and had a limited scripting language, making it a faulty foundation for Web3.
points: Bitcoin transactions were slow due to limited block size and time-consuming mining.
The Bitcoin blockchain only recorded BTC and could not accommodate other cryptoassets.
The Bitcoin scripting language was too simplistic for generalized applications.
He proposed a superior foundational protocol allowing others to build decentralized applications on top.

q: What is ether (ETH) and what role does it play on the Ethereum network?
lead: Ether (ETH) is Ethereum's native token, described by Buterin as the internal crypto fuel used to buy the 'gas' required to run dapps.
points: The amount of gas depends on the computational resources a program requires.
This system incentivizes developers to write efficient applications and deters malicious behavior.
ETH was verified via proof-of-work until September 2022, then proof-of-stake.
There is no cap on ETH; around 120 million were in circulation in April 2022.

q: What is a smart contract? What is a decentralized application (dapp)? How are smart contracts and dapps related to LEGO?
lead: A smart contract is computer code that executes a transaction without human intervention, while a dapp is a web application built on decentralized P2P infrastructure.
points: Smart contracts are encrypted on the blockchain to prevent alterations, removing financial intermediaries.
Smart contracts are the LEGO pieces; dapps and DAOs are what you build with them.
Smart contracts can be written in Solidity, a Turing-complete language running on the EVM.
Many dapps combine a smart contract with a front-end user interface.

q: What is a token and what are the different categories used by dapps?
lead: A token is a digital asset whose ownership is recorded on a blockchain, categorized broadly as fungible (ERC-20) or non-fungible (ERC-721).
points: Fungible tokens include payment, utility (service), security, and governance tokens.
Payment tokens like ETH buy things; utility tokens like BAT claim a service.
Governance tokens grant voting rights, such as the Maker token for DAI decisions.
Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) represent unique, indivisible items like art or collectibles.

q: What is a decentralized autonomous organization (DAO) and what happened to the one created by Slock.It?
lead: A DAO is a virtual organization operating by rules coded in smart contracts; Slock.It's DAO was hacked and drained of ETH.
points: Slock.It launched the first DAO on April 29, 2016, raising $150 million from 11,000 members.
Hackers siphoned off $40 million of ETH on June 17, 2016.
The response sparked a debate over blockchain immutability and a decisive vote.
It culminated in a hard fork splitting Ethereum (ETH) and Ethereum Classic (ETC).

q: What is the purpose of a stablecoin and what are the different types? What distinguishes Tether from DAI?
lead: A stablecoin is a payment token kept stable by pegging to another asset, solving cryptocurrency volatility; Tether is centralized while DAI is decentralized.
points: Types are backed by fiat currency, cryptocurrencies, commodities, or nothing (algorithmic).
Tether (USDT) is a centralized coin controlled by the founders of the Bitfinex exchange.
DAI is a decentralized stablecoin controlled by the MakerDAO smart contract.
Stablecoins are typically pegged at $1.00 and backed by collateral.

q: What is decentralized finance (DeFi) and what are the main activities across different blockchains?
lead: DeFi is a movement to build a global, decentralized financial system on cryptoassets, blockchains, and smart contracts, disintermediating traditional financial intermediaries.
points: It creates a system that is open, permissionless, transparent, and automated.
Biggest categories are DEXes, borrowing/lending, and liquidity staking.
Ethereum dominates with close to 60% of TVL, followed by Tron (10.8%) and BSC (10.5%).
Other activities include yield farming, bridge tokens, CDPs, and derivatives.

q: What is total value locked and how it is measured?
lead: Total value locked (TVL) is the dollar value of cryptocurrencies locked in smart contracts, liquidity pools, or staked on a PoS blockchain, and is the main measure of DeFi market size.
points: TVL is the most widely used measure of the health of crypto.
The DeFi market was close to $40 billion at year-end 2022, down from $175 billion.
DeFi represented 5% of all cryptocurrencies' $800 billion market capitalization.
Figures are tracked by the website DefiLlama, as no official statistics exist.

q: Which DeFi activities do you find most interesting and why?
lead: The chapter presents several notable DeFi activities including decentralized exchanges, collateralized debt positions, liquidity staking, and derivatives, each demonstrating the 'crypto LEGO' potential.
points: DEXes like Curve use automated market makers and liquidity pools to swap tokens.
MakerDAO acts as a decentralized central bank, minting stablecoin DAI against collateral.
Liquidity staking via Lido lets users double-dip returns using stTokens.
Synthetix derivatives give on-chain exposure to off-chain assets through 'synths'.
